behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: The core failure mode is grouping-key logic (trace ID vs service name). This can be exposed with in-memory log objects from multiple services and does not require I/O or module-boundary infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the grouping function with mixed-service log entries sharing trace IDs, including interleaved service names. Assert one group per trace ID and that each group contains entries from all emitting services for that trace.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Clock-skew handling usually sits at the boundary between correlation/normalization and timeline ordering. A pure sort unit test can miss failures in how per-service offsets are applied before rendering.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser-normalized events + skew-correction + timeline builder in-process. Feed two-service traces with known +/-500ms skew and expected causal order. Assert final timeline order matches corrected event sequence.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often occur in percentile computation + baseline retrieval/windowing + report flagging wiring. Testing only the threshold formula in isolation can miss end-to-end anomaly flag propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the metrics aggregation and anomaly-report modules together on synthetic latency streams with a stable baseline then an injected spike where p99 exceeds 3x. Assert anomaly flag appears in the produced report with the correct metric context.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about parser error handling interacting with pipeline continuation and reporting counters. A parser-only unit test would not prove pipeline survival and aggregate skipped-count reporting.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the ingest->parse->correlate/report pipeline in-process with a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON lines. Assert processing completes, valid records continue through, and skipped-entry count equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: This is a throughput/backpressure claim that depends on runtime scheduling, buffering, and process behavior under load. Mocked or purely unit/integration setups can hide real drops/lag.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real tool process with a high-rate log generator producing 10,000 entries/sec for a fixed interval. Measure accepted vs emitted counts, queue/backlog growth, and end-to-end processing lag; fail on any sustained deficit or dropped-entry mismatch.
